purpose: Document the pressure-decomposition misunderstanding and update the July 8 presentation slide to use a mechanical-pressure chart rather than the signed p_rgh density-gradient decomposition as the primary visual.
interpretation: The signed p_rgh density-gradient decomposition is useful as a method/audit figure but is not presentation-safe as a mechanical pressure-loss comparison.
The right leg is the downcomer and flows downward with gravity.
The left_lower_leg, test_section_span, and left_upper_leg form the riser/upcomer path and flow upward against gravity.
The upper leg does not dominate mechanical pressure loss; its large signed density-gradient value is a p_rgh cancellation/source term.
